      "それはサーフィン、ボディボーディング、スレイイング、スキムボード、スノーボード、ウェイクボードの要素を持っています。",
      "それは「シートウェーブ」と呼ばれる人工の波を持っています。",
      "それは人工の波の上でバランスを取ろうとするライダーを持っています。",
      "それはトリックを決めようとするライダーを持っています。",
      "それは定常的な波を持っています。",
      "それは定常的な表面上を流れる水からライダーの動きが得られることを特徴としています。",
      "それはパッド付きの傾斜面を持っています。",
      "それは湾曲した表面を水が上昇する薄い水のシートを持っています。",
      "それは試乗のためのミニチュアFlowRiderを持っています。",
      "それはライダーが滑るのを防ぐための真空装置を持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは定常的な波を特徴としています。",
      "それは、海の波の形を再現するように設計された表面の上を水がポンプで送られることを特徴としています。",
      "それは、定常的な表面上を流れる水からライダーの動きが得られることを特徴としています。",
      "それは、毎秒410,000リットル以上の水を噴射する技術を特徴としています。",
      "それは、満員時に最大1.8メートルの高さの波を特徴としています。",
      "それは、人工の波の上でバランスを取ろうとするライダーを特徴としています。",
      "それは、浅い波を通る水の動きを特徴としています。",
      "それは、ブレークする波のシミュレーションを特徴としています。",
      "それは、実際の波よりもはるかに大きな水の流速を特徴としています。",
      "それは、ライダーが表面を危険に滑るのを防ぐための真空装置を特徴としています。"
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "それは2012年にユタ州で開催されました。",
      "それは2016年にシンガポールで開催されました。"

About: It is an alternative boardsport incorporating elements of surfing, bodyboarding, slaying, skimboarding, snowboarding and wakeboarding.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with FlowRider technology.
CreatedBy: It was created by Tom Lochtefeld.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Tom Lochtefeld.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Tom Lochtefeld.
HasA: It has elements of surfing, bodyboarding, slaying, skimboarding, snowboarding and wakeboarding.
It has artificial waves called "sheet waves".
It has a rider who attempts to balance on artificial waves.
It has a rider who seeks to perform tricks.
It has a stationary wave.
It has a rider's movement derived from water flowing over a stationary surface.
It has a padded, inclined surface.
It has a thin sheet of water shooting up a curved surface.
It has miniature FlowRiders for trials.
It has a vacuum to keep riders from skipping.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a stationary wave.
It is characterized by water pumped up and over a surface engineered to replicate an ocean wave.
It is characterized by the movement of the rider derived from the water flowing over the stationary surface.
It is characterized by a technology that shoots more than 410,000 L of water per second.
It is characterized by waves as high as 1.8 m at full capacity.
It is characterized by riders attempting to balance on artificial waves.
It is characterized by the upward motion of water through a shallow wave.
It is characterized by the simulation of breaking waves.
It is characterized by a flow rate of water much larger than an actual wave.
It is characterized by a vacuum that helps keep riders from skipping.
HeldIn: It was held in Utah in 2012.
It was held in Singapore in 2016.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by observing waters seemingly flowing up a wave.
It was influenced by seeing surfers ride shallow waves without touching the ocean floor.
IsA: It is a late 20th century alternative boardsport.
KnownFor: It is known for incorporating elements of surfing, bodyboarding, slaying, skimboarding, snowboarding and wakeboarding.
It is known for artificial waves called "sheet waves".
It is known for the FlowRider and FlowBarrel technologies.
It is known for the Flowriding League Of the World (FLOW).
It is known for the World Flowboarding Championships (WFC).
OccurredIn: It occurred in the late 20th century.
PerformedBy: It was performed by flow riders.
It was performed by professional riders.
It was performed by men's Flowboarders.
It was performed by female Flowboarders.
ProducedBy: It was produced by FlowRider, Inc.
UsedAs: It is used as a sport.
It is used as a promotional tour.
It is used as a simulation of breaking waves.
UsedFor: It is used for mimicking movements of other board sports.
It is used for performing tricks.
It is used for competitive level wave structures.
It is used for simulating different forms of ocean waves.
It is used for representing a region in the World Flowboarding Championships.
UsedIn: It is used in water parks.
It is used in cruise ships.
It is used in Wave House venues.
UsedTo: It is used to create a stationary wave.
It is used to replicate the shape of an ocean wave.
It is used to mimic the movements of other board sports.
It is used to perform tricks.
It is used to simulate different forms of ocean waves.
It is used to provide a more realistic wave.
It is used to keep riders from skipping dangerously along the surface of the water.
WonBy: It was won by Bill Bryan.
